metadata.title:
SIX LOSE LIFE IN


G. n. CATASTROPHE

pages.raw_text:
cars that lie on the bottom of the
lake. The engine Is No. $40, with
a weight of 272,000 pounds, and is
supposed to lie in 100 feet Of
water.


When the accident occurred the
engine, mail, smoker and baggage
carsrent down 20 foot embank-
ment into the lake. The day car
remained, projecting from the en-
bankment into the water. The
sleeper remained within the portal
of the tunnel, while the dining and
tourist cars never left the track.
The acetylene tank under the
smoker exploded and set fire to
the wreckage. Some of the pas
sengers in this car, which was
partly submerged, escaped by
smashing windows and diving
through the apertures thus made.


The list of killed given out at the
Great Northern office today is as
follows:


N. Edward Munson, engineer,
Hillyard; Frank Bell, fireman,
Hillyard; Charles Danner, mail
clerk, Spokane; George r. Stick
land, express messenger; George
Howard Curtice, Spokane, lumber
man; T. J. Dolbow, Spokane.


E. NJ. Newcomb OF 1zO Indiana
avenue is reported missing.


The seriously injured are: C..
Mihills, Spokane; John Lord, Seat
tie, actor; J. Durbin, Spokane;
Tom Wainch, Spokane; Elmer E..
Hall, Colfax, Wash; Henry e.
Byoeum, Minot, N. D.; Thomas Hol-
land, Usk, Wash; M. E. Cash,
Standpoint, Idaho; Walter H. Ross
/ news agent, Seattle; E. B. New.
combe, Spokane; Pete Nelson,
Nagle hote, Spokane; F. d. Row
ans, walter; William Smith, wait.
er; w. q. Patterson, dining car
conductor; G. L. Thomas, New
port.
